trials.supervisor_analysis: Baseline established at score 60.0 (~0.95ms, 1.01x speedup) with profiling showing GEMM at 96.4% of runtime. The critical optimization opportunity is algebraically fusing avgpool into the matmul weights, reducing the GEMM from 8192x8192 to 8192x512 (16x less compute).

nudges.text: The avgpool can be algebraically fused into the matmul weights: since `avgpool(x @ W^T + b, 16)` is linear, you can precompute `W_pooled = W.reshape(512, 16, 8192).mean(dim=1)` and `b_pooled = b.reshape(512, 16).mean(dim=1)`, then do a much smaller matmul `(1024, 8192) @ (8192, 512)` instead of `(1024, 8192) @ (8192, 8192)`. This reduces the GEMM by 16x, directly attacking the 96% bottleneck.
